name: Hiking Cerro Jabalcón from Zújar around the Embalse de Negratín
name_original: Rodenado el Cerro Jabalcón desde Zújar por los alrededores del Embalse de Negratín
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Zújar traverse a diverse landscape in the Granada province of Andalusia, Spain. The region features the prominent Jabalcón Mountain, reaching 1,496 meters, alongside the expansive Negratín Reservoir. Terrain varies from arid flatlands and red canyons to lush, well-irrigated valleys and plateaus carved by water. These geographical features offer a range of conditions for touring cyclists. Best touring cycling routes around Zújar The most popular touring cycling route is Hiking Cerro Jabalcón from Zújar around the Embalse de Negratín, a 11.8 miles (19.1 km) trail that takes 1 hour 38 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers views of the Negratín Reservoir and the surrounding landscape. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Gorafe Desert – Gorafe Desert loop from Playa de Freila, a difficult 34.8 miles (56.0 km) path. This route explores the unique desert landscape, featuring varied terrain. Local touring cyclists also love the Castillo de Freila – Zújar loop from Cerro Capallón, a 19.0 miles (30.6 km) trail leading through the area surrounding Castillo de Freila, often completed in about 2 hours 4 minutes. Touring cycling around Zújar is defined by the Jabalcón Mountain, the Negratín Reservoir, and the Carramaiza Flatlands. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to different ability levels. The routes in Zújar are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.9 stars from more than 40 reviews. More than 500 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Zújar's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Zújar?
A: There are over 80 touring cycling routes around Zújar documented on komoot. These routes cater to various skill levels, with options ranging from easy to difficult.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Zújar?
A: Zújar offers a diverse range of terrain for touring cyclists. You'll find routes traversing arid flatlands, red and ochre canyons, and lush, well-irrigated valleys. The prominent Jabalcón Mountain provides challenging ascents, while the expansive Negratín Reservoir offers more serene, often hard-packed earth paths. Expect a mix of paved and semi-gravel sections, undulating terrain, and varying gradients.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or a relaxed ride?
A: While many routes in Zújar are rated moderate to difficult, there are 5 easy touring cycling routes available. These are ideal for beginners or those looking for a more relaxed experience, allowing you to enjoy the scenery without significant physical challenge.

Q: Can I find circular touring cycling routes in Zújar?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes in Zújar are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. An example of a moderate circular route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/27625806" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Negratín Dam – View of Negratín Reservoir loop from Playa de Freila&lt;/a&gt;, which offers stunning views of the reservoir.

Q: What natural landmarks and viewpoints can I discover along the cycling routes?
A: The Zújar area is rich in natural landmarks and offers spectacular viewpoints. You can cycle past the expansive Negratín Reservoir, explore the unique Gorafe Desert Canyons, or enjoy panoramic views from various points like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3128110" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views from Horro Mano de Hierro&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6484888" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Negratín Reservoir Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;. The Jabalcón Mountain itself provides commanding vistas of the surrounding landscape.

Q: Are there any family-friendly touring cycling options in Zújar?
A: For families, the 'Old railway gravel path: Vía Verde, Baza - Baúl' is an excellent option. This greenway is designed for walkers and cyclists, offering a relatively flat and scenic route through a desert landscape, featuring impressive railway bridges and old train stations. It provides a safe and enjoyable experience for all ages.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Zújar?
A: The best time for touring cycling in Zújar generally depends on your preference for heat. Spring (April-May) and Autumn (September-October) offer pleasant temperatures, making them ideal for exploring. Summers can be very hot, especially in the arid flatlands, while winters are milder but can have cooler mornings.

Q: Are there any unique attractions or activities near the cycling routes?
A: Absolutely! After a ride, you can relax and rejuvenate in the natural warm thermal waters of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5311664" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Baths of Negratín (Zújar thermal pool)&lt;/a&gt;, located next to the Negratín reservoir. You can also visit historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3241591" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Baúl Bridge&lt;/a&gt; or explore the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2793795" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorafe Desert Canyons&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Zújar?
A: The touring cycling routes in Zújar are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.9 stars from over 40 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, stunning views of the Negratín Reservoir, and the unique landscapes of the Gorafe Desert.

Q: Can I find challenging touring cycling routes in Zújar?
A: Yes, Zújar offers numerous challenging routes for experienced touring cyclists. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38175222" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jabalcón Geodetic Vertex – Lancha del Romero loop from Monasterio La Granja&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 38.6 km route with significant elevation gain. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31402018" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorafe Desert – Gorafe Desert loop from Playa de Freila&lt;/a&gt;, which explores the unique desert landscape over 56 km.

Q: Are there any routes that offer views of the Negratín Reservoir?
A: Many routes provide stunning views of the Negratín Reservoir. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1059358742" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hiking Cerro Jabalcón from Zújar around the Embalse de Negratín&lt;/a&gt; is a popular difficult route that offers continuous views of the reservoir and the surrounding landscape. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/27625806" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Negratín Dam – View of Negratín Reservoir loop from Playa de Freila&lt;/a&gt; is another excellent moderate option for lakeside scenery.
tips: Great views over Embaise de Negratín and the 'Badlands' behind it.

The town of Zújar is a hidden gem in the middle of nature. Known for its impressive reservoir and its location on the slopes of the Sierra de Baza, Zújar is an ideal destination for those seeking tranquility and contact with nature. Its narrow, cobbled streets and its typical Andalusian white houses give it a special charm.

waypoints.name: View of Zújar and Cerro Jabalcón
